fields.description:
## Why
The auto-lander is a long-running service; it must deploy like the review-bot and log to its OWN service logs — writing to `session_log` would blur that type's purpose (it is for agentic sessions, not CI/infra processes).

## What
Containerize the service and wire it into docker-compose (single instance) on the existing Gerrit box; route logs to container stdout → the existing CloudWatch monitoring.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S2** (`perovskite-frilly-grebe`, 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec) — the core loop: this story packages and deploys the loop service, so there must be a service to containerize; the deploy is otherwise independent of the failure/command stories.

## Scope
Deploy + observability + single-instance guard, **and the service-lifecycle wiring the deployed process needs** — heartbeat emission (AC5), the queue-status/observability surface (AC6), and emergency-stop + drain-on-shutdown (AC7). The **core rebase/submit loop logic** (selection, rebase, Verified-await, submit, `wipChain`) is S2's; the heartbeat write, queue-status read, sentinel check, and SIGTERM drain are thin hooks *into* that loop that this deploy story owns because they are operational/lifecycle concerns, and they live in the same service module (`infra/autolander/loop.py`). This resolves the apparent "not the loop logic" tension: S5 owns *when/where the service runs and how it is observed and controlled*, not *how it rebases and submits*.

## Files
New files:
- `infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander` — service image + `HEALTHCHECK`.
- `infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf` — the `autolander_errors` alarm.

Existing files edited:
- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` — add the `autolander` compose service (single instance) **and** declare its named state volume as `external: true` (the only volume pattern in this stack), holding the flock lockfile (AC1) and the heartbeat file (AC5).
- `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` — add the autolander state volume to `SITE_SUBDIRS` (the single source of truth for provisioning external volumes as EBS-backed bind-mounts under `/var/gerrit/site/*`). **This is mandatory:** `config-check.sh` check 5 (the incident-2731 drift gate) hard-fails CI when a `docker-compose.yml` `external: true` volume is absent from `SITE_SUBDIRS`, and `compose up` errors at runtime without it.
- `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` — add `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` to the `local.rebar_secret_params` set consumed by the existing `for_each` `aws_ssm_parameter` resource (which carries `ignore_changes = [value]` + the CHANGEME sentinel), NOT as a standalone resource (a standalone resource without `ignore_changes` would revert the operator-set value on every apply).
- `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` — add the leaf→env mapping `autolander-gerrit-token → AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`, materialized into the SAME shared `.env` the other compose services read (distinct env-var name from the review-bot's `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN`, since this is a different bot identity).
- `infra/scripts/observability.sh` — add the `autolander_errors` `PutMetricData` feeders.
- `infra/gerrit/` provisioning — the `All-Projects` `refs/meta/config` `[capability]` grant (`rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader`) + submit on `refs/heads/*` is a NEW integration surface (`setup-project.sh` today manages only the `rebar` project's `refs/meta/config`, and no `[capability]` block exists yet in the repo). It needs a named provisioning step (extend `setup-project.sh` or add `infra/gerrit/setup-all-projects.sh`) — the exact `[capability]`/`[access]` stanza and provisioning path is the operator-cutover surface (admin-only push to `All-Projects`).

Read-only / unchanged (referenced, not edited): `infra/terraform/iam.tf` — its existing `ssm_params_read` grant on the `rebar/prod/*` prefix already covers the token path, so NO IAM change is needed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander` (new) + a compose service added to the existing `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`.
- [ ] **Single-instance guard:** the container acquires a POSIX advisory **file lock** (`flock` on a lockfile in its state volume) on start; a second copy fails to acquire it and exits. This `flock` is the **sole** single-instance mechanism — do NOT rely on `deploy.replicas: 1` (a Swarm-only key silently ignored by plain `docker compose`).
- [ ] **Credentials (AC2 mechanism):** `autolander-bot`/RebarBotNava's `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` is a **server-level capability** granted in `All-Projects` `refs/meta/config` (`[capability]` / service-user grant), not a per-project label ACL; plus submit permission on `refs/heads/*`. The Gerrit HTTP token is provisioned as an SSM **SecureString** at the concrete path `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` — inside the existing `parameter/rebar/prod/*` prefix that the EC2 instance-role IAM policy (`iam.tf` `ssm_params_read`) already grants, so NO IAM change is needed (a `/rebar/autolander/*` path would fall outside that grant) — added in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`, fetched by `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`; never inlined.
- [ ] **Observability (AC3):** logs go to container stdout; `infra/scripts/observability.sh` publishes the single `rebar/host:autolander_errors` metric (`PutMetricData`) from BOTH feeders — the `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` journald marker (a structured stdout marker the process itself writes, matching the existing `VOTER_ERROR`/`G2P` pattern) AND the container's `unhealthy` state, with an alarm in `infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf`. **Factual correction:** the container `unhealthy` state is a Docker health-state transition — a **Docker engine event**, NOT a stdout line in journald under `CONTAINER_NAME`; the existing grep-journald pattern will never match it. The unhealthy feeder must therefore poll `docker inspect --format '{{.State.Health.Status}}'` (or subscribe to `docker events`), a different probe shape — the concrete probe is settled together with the AC4 restart mechanism. The service NEVER writes `session_log`.
- [ ] **Liveness/restart (AC4):** a `HEALTHCHECK` in `Dockerfile.autolander` marks the container `unhealthy` on a **hang** by probing the **local heartbeat file freshness** (stat the AC5 heartbeat in the state volume; unhealthy if older than the staleness threshold) — it MUST NOT probe Gerrit reachability, or a Gerrit outage would mark the container unhealthy and drive a self-inflicted restart flap that also increments `autolander_errors`. A plain `restart: unless-stopped` only fires on process EXIT, not on a HEALTHCHECK `unhealthy` transition — so recovering from a *hang* requires an explicit mechanism (**design decision, see below**); the chosen mechanism recreates the container on `unhealthy`, and the `unhealthy` state feeds the same `autolander_errors` metric named in AC3. (The `wipChain`-timeout parking of a stuck stack is loop logic owned by S2/S3, not this deploy story.)
- [ ] **Heartbeat (AC5):** the bot writes a **heartbeat** — a timestamp file in the state volume (or a health metric) — on **every poll** iteration, i.e. once per the bot's **15 s poll interval** (S2's loop cadence), so under normal operation the heartbeat is never older than ~15 s and a stale one means the bot is down/wedged. **Contract (design-fixed):** heartbeat freshness ≤ 15 s; S4's `lander_down` treats a heartbeat **older than 90 s** (6× the poll) as down. This heartbeat (its location + the 90 s staleness threshold) is the interface contract S4's `lander_down` outcome reads.
- [ ] **Queue-status surface (AC6):** emit a small queue-status surface exposing the current `wipChain` (change, phase, `tested_sha`, time-in-phase), the count of `Autosubmit`-set-and-waiting changes, and a **queue-wait / time-to-land histogram** metric with **design-fixed bucket boundaries of 30 s, 1 m, 2 m, 5 m, 10 m, 20 m, 30 m, 1 h** (in seconds: 30, 60, 120, 300, 600, 1200, 1800, 3600) — spanning a green fast-forward landing (~1–2 min for the CI re-run on the rebased patchset) through a heavily-contended `N=5` re-drive, so p50/p90/p99 time-to-land are readable — for debuggability and to validate the success criterion.
- [ ] **Emergency-stop + drain (AC7):** an **emergency-stop** sentinel transitions the bot to the **`paused`** state (the `paused` value now in S2's `wipChain` `phase` enum) that STOPS consuming `Autosubmit` (for when `main` is on fire). The sentinel's concrete form + lifecycle (create/retire) is a **design decision, see below**. **Drain-on-shutdown:** on redeploy/SIGTERM, finish the current `wipChain`, **ack its outcome** (write the S3 outcome marker for the in-flight stack so the record is durable across the restart), then exit — so a redeploy never strands a half-processed stack. The drain is **time-bounded** (a SIGTERM drain deadline, e.g. matched to the CI-wait timeout): if the in-flight `wipChain` is still blocked on CI past the deadline, record its state and exit anyway rather than blocking the redeploy indefinitely (systemd/compose would SIGKILL regardless).
- [ ] **Verification/tests:** an **end-to-end proving step** boots the container against the real Gerrit 3.14.1 instance and confirms it authenticates (SSM token → `.env` → HTTP), writes a heartbeat, and logs ≥1 poll iteration — this is a first-time production cutover, so at least one observable live check is required (mock-only ACs would pass on paper while leaving the real boundary unexercised). Plus: the `flock` single-instance guard (a second copy exits), the HEALTHCHECK marks unhealthy when the heartbeat goes stale, the emergency-stop sentinel halts `Autosubmit` consumption, and drain-on-shutdown records the in-flight outcome. Recorded as `set_verify_commands` DD entries.
